# CI liveness allowlist for the A6.3 URL check (lint.py).
#
# URLs/hosts listed here are downgraded from an A6.3 "dead URL" VIOLATION to a
# stderr WARNING. Use this ONLY for sources that are genuinely live but return
# 4xx/5xx specifically to GitHub Actions runners (geo/IP blocks, bot WAFs that
# answer HEAD/GET from datacenter IPs with 403/500, etc.).
#
# Rules for adding an entry:
#   - Verify the URL is actually live from a normal workstation first
#     (HEAD and GET both succeed). If it is truly dead, fix the reference
#     instead of allowlisting it.
#   - Add a one-line comment with the date and the observed CI failure code.
#   - Prefer a bare host (covers all paths) over an exact URL when the whole
#     origin flakes; use an exact URL to scope the exception narrowly.
#
# Syntax: one entry per line. A line beginning with http:// or https:// is an
# exact-URL match; anything else is treated as a host (matches that host and
# its subdomains). '#' starts a comment.

# 2026-06-04: CHAI (Center for Human-Compatible AI) homepage returns HTTP 500
# to GitHub Actions runners but serves 200 (HEAD+GET) from a workstation.
# Cited by cooperative-preference-inference, corrigible-off-switch-incentive,
# preference-uncertain-agent.
humancompatible.ai

# 2026-06-04: Elsevier DOIs resolve (doi.org 302 -> linkinghub.elsevier.com) but
# linkinghub answers HEAD/GET from GitHub Actions runners with HTTP 405; both
# verified live (302 -> real PII) from a workstation. j.jss.2024.112278 is the
# "Agent design pattern catalogue" paper cited by ~18 patterns; j.jml.2005.08.005
# ("Memory and the self") is cited by self-archaeology.
https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jss.2024.112278
https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jml.2005.08.005

# 2026-06-14: hosts verified live from a workstation but timing out / refusing
# from GitHub Actions runners during the global-scan reference enrichment.
# ijcesen.com (TLS handshake timeout) cited by silent-source-rot;
# cmegroup.com (read timeout) cited by velocity-magnitude-governor;
# people.idsia.ch (read timeout) cited by open-question-tension-store;
# datasociety.net hosts the Moral Crumple Zones paper for accountability-laundering-via-algorithm.
ijcesen.com
cmegroup.com
people.idsia.ch
datasociety.net

# 2026-06-17: welivesecurity.com (ESET research blog, behind Cloudflare) returns
# HTTP 503 to GitHub Actions runners but serves the full article (HEAD+GET 200)
# from a normal workstation. Cited by guardrail-erosion-through-compaction
# (OpenClaw computer-use assistant analysis).
welivesecurity.com
